revision.comment: Text replacement - "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA ==" to "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Agatanushi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Agata-nushi Jinja in Minokamo2008-1.jpg |location = Nishimachi 7-chome, [[Minokamo City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}} |coordinates = {{Coord|35.452583|N|137.000278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |Deities = [[Hikoimasu no Mikoto]] |Rank = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha]] (small shrine), Former Gō-sha, [[Kinpei-sha]] |Established = Unknown |Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] (Single-bay Nagare-zukuri) |Festivals = First Sunday in April |location = {{ill|Minokamo|qid=Q857305|1=Minokamo, Gifu}} |architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} }} '''Agatanushi Shrine''' (縣主神社, Agatanushi Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Minokamo City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}, in the former [[Ōta Town (Gifu)|Ōta Town]] of [[Kamo District (Gifu)|Kamo District]]. Sometimes written as '''県主神社''', it is one of the "Three Kamo Shrines" (三加茂神社 or 三賀茂神社), alongside [[Kamo Shrine (Minokamo)|Kamo Shrine]] in Minokamo City and {{ill|Sakahogi Shrine|ja|坂祝神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakahogi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakahogi Shrine|1=Sakahogi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11425559}} in [[Sakahogi Town]]. == Deities == * {{ill|8=ja|9=彦坐王|Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi|lt=Hikoimasu|lt=Hikoimasu no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/8=ja|qq=}} (彦坐王命) Hikoimasu no Mikoto was a [[Prince of Emperor Kaika]]. Before the [[Ritsuryō system]], Kamo District was a direct territory ([[Ancient Japan's local government|Agata]]) governed by the [[Yamato Court]]'s official [[Kamo Agatanushi (Imperial Line)|Kamo Agatanushi]], who is considered an ancestor of Hikoimasu no Mikoto<ref name="SurveyReport">"Shikinaisha Survey Report".</ref>. The deity is believed to have powers related to [[:en:rain rituals|rain rituals]]<ref name="SurveyReport"/>. == History == The shrine's exact founding date is unknown. According to tradition, it was established by the Kamo Agatanushi to worship their ancestor Hikoimasu no Mikoto<ref>"Shrine Directory", Association of Shinto Shrines, 1963.</ref>. Another theory suggests that Hikoimasu no Mikoto himself ruled this area, and his descendants, who became the Agatanushi, founded the shrine. Nearby, there are cluster tombs ([[gunshūfun]]), which are thought to have been constructed by the Kamo Agatanushi clan during the area's early development<ref name="SurveyReport"/>. Hikoimasu no Mikoto is identified as the father of [[Kamiosu no Mikoto]], an ancestor of the [[Mototsu Kokuzō]], a powerful clan in {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|qid=Q864156|lt=Mino Province|1=Mino Province}}. Kamiosu no Mikoto is also associated with [[Mino Kokuzō]] and [[Muge Kokuzō]], indicating close ties between the rulers of Kamo, Mototsu, and Muge Districts<ref>"Records of Emperor Kaika", Kojiki.</ref><ref>"Nihon Shoki", Emperor Keikō 4th year.</ref>. The shrine was reportedly rebuilt during the {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} era (859–877 CE) by [[Arihara no Yukihira]], a [[Governor of Mino Province]]. However, this may confuse Yukihira with his brother, {{ill|Ariwara no Narihira|en|Ariwara no Narihira|qid=Q451991|lt=Ariwara no Narihira|1=Ariwara no Narihira}}, who served as Deputy Governor of Mino in 880 CE<ref name="SurveyReport"/>. The shrine is listed as a [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. During the [[Late Heian period]], the shrine was designated "Second Rank Agatanushi Ōkami" in the [[Kokushiryō]] (Records of the Nation's Gods), confirming its importance within Kamo District<ref name="SurveyReport"/>. Later, during the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, the shrine became revered by the [[Toki clan]], rulers of [[Ōkō Castle]], and was considered the total shrine (Sōja) of Kamo District. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it was known as "Kamo Daimyōjin." In [[:en:Meiji 6|Meiji 6]] (1873), the shrine was renamed to its current name and Ranked as a Gōsha. It was designated as a shrine for providing [[Shinsen Heihaku]] (Imperial offerings) in 1907. == Festivals == The shrine's main festival was traditionally held on September 9 and featured grand ceremonies, including [[mikoshi]] processions and {{ill|Horse racing|en|Horse racing|qid=Q187916|lt=Horse racing|1=Horse racing}}. However, it fell into disuse over time. In the {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa era|1=Shōwa era}}, the date was changed to April 4, and from 1977 onward, it has been held on the first Sunday in April. Today, it involves joint celebrations with six shrines in the former Ōta Town<ref name="SurveyReport"/>. == Access == * Approximately 10 minutes on foot from [[:en:Nagaragawa Railway|Nagaragawa Railway]]'s [[:en:Maehirakōen Station|Maehirakōen Station]]. * Steps away from the "Agatanushi Shrine Entrance" bus stop on the [[Ai Ai Bus]] Kamo Line (operating on Mondays and Thursdays). == References == {{reflist|qq=}} == Further Reading == * ''Shikinaisha Survey Report'', Vol. 13, Tōsandō 2, Kōgakkan University Press, 1986. {{shinto-stub|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|縣主神社 (美濃加茂市)|version=99022311|comment=Imported full ja history}}
<!--Agatanushi Shrine (Minokamo)--><!--Agatanushi Shrine (Minokamo)--><!--Agatanushi Shrine (Minokamo)--><!--Agatanushi Shrine (Minokamo)--><!--Agatanushi Shrine (Minokamo)--><!--Agatanushi Shrine (Minokamo)--><!--Agatanushi Shrine (Minokamo)--><!--Agatanushi Shrine (Minokamo)--><!--Agatanushi Shrine (Minokamo)--><!--Agatanushi Shrine (Minokamo)--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Agatanushi-jinja (Minokamo)}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]
'''Agatanushi Shrine (Minokamo) (Q11608132)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mino Province]].
== Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Kamo District, Gifu|en|Kamo District, Gifu|ja|加茂郡|ceb|Kamo-gun (distrito sa Hapon, Gifu-ken)|es|Distrito de Kamo (Gifu)|fr|District de Kamo (Gifu)|id|Distrik Kamo, Gifu|it|Distretto di Kamo (Gifu)|ko|가모군 (기후현)|pl|Powiat Kamo|pt|Distrito de Kamo|th|อำเภอคาโมะ|uk|Повіт Камо (Ґіфу)|vi|Kamo (quận)|zh|加茂郡|lt=Kamo district|qid=Q1348656|1=Kamo District, Gifu}} || {{ill|Agatanushi Shrine (Minokamo)|lt=Agatanushi Shrine (Minokamo)|ja|縣主神社 (美濃加茂市)|commons|Category:Agatanushi-jinja (Minokamo)|qid=Q11608132|1=Agatanushi Shrine (Minokamo)}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || — || — || {{coord|35.452415|137.000264|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181949 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11608132}} [[da:d:Q11608132]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mino Province]]==Notes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
